summary: See clues file for full details. 3 tossups, 15 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 3 tossups and 15 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: This artist depicted men in horizontal profile stacked in bunks in Shipping out to Victory, the first of a War Series heavy on brown and black tempera.
examples: This artist depicted men in horizontal profile stacked in bunks in Shipping out to Victory, the first of a War Series heavy on brown and black tempera.

clues.clue: Historical quotations like "is life so dear or peace so sweet..." title entries in another series by this artist, which is currently touring in the "American Struggle" exhibition.
examples: Historical quotations like "is life so dear or peace so sweet..." title entries in another series by this artist, which is currently touring in the "American Struggle" exhibition.

clues.clue: This artist's wife, Gwendolyn Knight, edited his often-didactic titles.
examples: This artist's wife, Gwendolyn Knight, edited his often-didactic titles.

clues.clue: Colorful New York tenements inspired the style of this "Dynamic Cubist," who created silkscreen prints on the Legend of John Brown.
examples: Colorful New York tenements inspired the style of this "Dynamic Cubist," who created silkscreen prints on the Legend of John Brown.

clues.clue: This artist depicted three girls in red, yellow, and blue at a blackboard and included a blue and brown lattice above a crowd of people funneling into doors for "St.
examples: This artist depicted three girls in red, yellow, and blue at a blackboard and included a blue and brown lattice above a crowd of people funneling into doors for "St.

clues.clue: Louis," "Chicago," and "New York." For 10 points, name this Harlem Renaissance artist of the Migration series.
examples: Louis," "Chicago," and "New York." For 10 points, name this Harlem Renaissance artist of the Migration series.

clues.clue: For one series, this artist prevented color variation in his homemade casein  tempera by painting all the panels in parallel one color at a time.
examples: For one series, this artist prevented color variation in his homemade casein  tempera by painting all the panels in parallel one color at a time.

clues.clue: Because of this artist's renown, he and his wife Gwendolyn Knight were not included in the Spiral Group.
examples: Because of this artist's renown, he and his wife Gwendolyn Knight were not included in the Spiral Group.

clues.clue: Mexican muralism influenced the "dynamic cubism" of a series by this artist with even and odd number panels split by the MoMA and the Phillips Collection.
examples: Mexican muralism influenced the "dynamic cubism" of a series by this artist with even and odd number panels split by the MoMA and the Phillips Collection.

clues.clue: A zigzagging golden rope divides the two sections of a flat, beige canvas depicting a (*) restaurant in a painting by this artist.
examples: A zigzagging golden rope divides the two sections of a flat, beige canvas depicting a (*) restaurant in a painting by this artist.

clues.clue: A series by this artist depicts three girls writing the numbers "2, 3, 4" on an otherwise empty chalkboard.
examples: A series by this artist depicts three girls writing the numbers "2, 3, 4" on an otherwise empty chalkboard.

clues.clue: He used long captions for a series that depicts a crowd of silhouettes filing through doors labeled "Chicago," "New York," and "St.
examples: He used long captions for a series that depicts a crowd of silhouettes filing through doors labeled "Chicago," "New York," and "St.
